exams.title: React & TypeScript Frontend Architecture (MCQ)
exams.description: Assessments on custom state sync, virtual DOM concepts, React 19 hooks, and TypeScript module resolution configurations.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is true regarding React 19 Server Actions?
options: They can only run on client-side environments.
They are functions designed to handle form submissions and data mutations directly on the server.
They completely replace Redux Toolkit for local global state management.
They must return HTML structures instead of serializable JSON objects.
questions.correctAnswer: They are functions designed to handle form submissions and data mutations directly on the server.

questions.questionText: In tsconfig.json, what is the primary benefit of enabling the moduleResolution: "bundler" flag?
options: It forces TypeScript to compile files to CommonJS format.
It configures the compiler to resolve imports modeled after modern frontend bundlers (like Vite or Webpack).
It disables all external type-checking to speed up builds.
It automatically bundles node_modules into a single production index file.
questions.correctAnswer: It configures the compiler to resolve imports modeled after modern frontend bundlers (like Vite or Webpack).

questions.questionText: What is the exact behavior of useMemo dependency checking in React 18+?
options: It performs deep recursive equality checks on all objects inside the dependency array.
It performs strict reference equality checks (Object.is) on the primitive or object values declared in the array.
It forces a component re-render even if the dependency values are completely identical.
It runs the memoized calculation asynchronously in a separate web worker thread.
questions.correctAnswer: It performs strict reference equality checks (Object.is) on the primitive or object values declared in the array.

questions.questionText: Which Tailwind v4 directive is the official syntax for importing Tailwind classes in index.css?
options: @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "tailwind/v4/all.css";
import 'tailwindcss' in main.tsx instead of css files.

exams.title: Advanced System Engineering & Cloud Infrastructure (All Types)
exams.description: Advanced topics covering relational Spanner database schemas, security rule hardening, and JWT authentication middleware setups.

questions.questionText: In the context of REST APIs, why is it critical to handle sensitive API keys exclusively server-side (e.g. in Express routes) instead of local browser components?
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent exposing private credentials, API tokens, and access rights in public client-side bundles accessible via DevTools.

questions.questionText: Which of the following describes the difference between symmetric and asymmetric token signing in JWT?
options: Asymmetric signing uses a single shared secret, while symmetric signing uses a public/private keypair.
Symmetric signing uses a single shared secret key, while asymmetric signing uses a public/private keypair (like RS256).
Asymmetric signing is strictly forbidden in modern web frameworks.
Symmetric signing is inherently slower because it requires network verification.
questions.correctAnswer: Symmetric signing uses a single shared secret key, while asymmetric signing uses a public/private keypair (like RS256).

questions.questionText: Describe how you would configure a Rate Limiter in an Express gateway. Detail the architectural reasons for placing this middleware before JWT validation.
questions.correctAnswer: Rate limiters must be placed before JWT verification to mitigate DoS/brute-force attacks on CPU-intensive decryption operations. Express-rate-limit or Redis stores are commonly configured.

questions.questionText: Programming Challenge: Write a JavaScript function that takes a positive integer and returns its factorial.
Architecture Submission: Upload your certified technical architect report or cloud system design diagram (PDF format preferred).
Verbal Presentation: Explain the key differences between ACID and BASE database properties in under 60 seconds.

answers.q1: They are functions designed to handle form submissions and data mutations directly on the server.
answers.q2: It configures the compiler to resolve imports modeled after modern frontend bundlers (like Vite or Webpack).
answers.q3: It performs strict reference equality checks (Object.is) on the primitive or object values declared in the array.
